Body:
P1: Hey, Arjun, how’s it going, man?
P2: Yo, Vikram, all good here! Just chilling after a long day at college. You?
P1: Same, yaar. Just finished a project for my engineering class. Brain is fried!
P2: Haha, I feel you. Which subject was it this time?
P1: Mechanics, bhai. So many calculations, I almost gave up.
P2: Been there! But you’re killing it, I’m sure. By the way, did you start prepping for placements?
P1: Not yet, man. I’m thinking of joining some coding classes. Companies are crazy about tech skills now.
P2: True that. I’m brushing up on Python myself. Let’s study together sometime, maybe over chai at that stall near Andheri?
P1: Deal! Chai and coding, best combo. How about next weekend?
P2: Works for me. Let’s plan closer to the date. Oh, and are you coming for the Diwali party at Rohan’s place?
P1: Wouldn’t miss it, yaar. Gotta get some new kurtas for that. You?
P2: Of course! Already got my outfit ready. Let’s catch up there too.
P1: Cool, bhai. I’ll call you later to confirm the chai plan.
P2: Sure, take care, man!

P1: Hey, Arjun, what’s up? Been a while!
P2: Hey, Meera! Yeah, yaar, just caught up with work. How are you?
P1: I’m good, thanks! Listen, I need a caterer for a small Diwali get-together. Any recommendations?
P2: Oh, for sure! There’s this amazing caterer in Thane I used for my sister’s birthday. They do awesome Indian food.
P1: That sounds great. Do they have veg and non-veg options?
P2: Yes, both. Their paneer tikka and biryani were a hit at our party.
P1: Wow, my mouth is watering already! How do I contact them?
P2: I’ll send you their contact. Just mention my name, they might give a discount.
P1: Thanks, Arjun, you’re a lifesaver! Hey, are you joining us for Diwali?
P2: Would love to, Meera! Just confirm the date, I’ll be there with crackers.
P1: Haha, awesome! It’s on the 10th. I’ll text you the details.
P2: Perfect. Looking forward to it. Talk soon!
P1: Take care, bye!
P2: Bye, Meera!

P1: Heyy, I’m thinking of a quick trip to Kerala during the long weekend.
P2: Whoa, Kerala? That’s gonna be so serene. Which weekend?
P1: The one in December, around Christmas. Want a break from the cold up here.
P2: I feel you. Delhi winters are brutal. Where in Kerala?
P1: Planning for Munnar. Those tea plantations are calling me!
P2: Amazing choice. The hills there are unreal. You going solo?
P1: Nah, with my college gang. We’re looking for homestays.
P2: That’s the best way to experience Kerala. Try the local fish curry, okay?
P1: Oh, for sure! I’ve heard it’s to die for. Any other tips?
P2: Yeah, take a shikara ride if you can. It’s super calming.
P1: Will do. Flights are pricey though, almost ₹20,000 return.
P2: Ouch, that’s steep. Maybe check for deals on MakeMyTrip?
P1: Good idea. I’ll look tonight. Wanna join if it’s not too expensive?
P2: Tempting, but I’ve got family stuff. Keep me updated though!
P1: Will do. I’ll send pics if we finalize. Talk later?
P2: Yeah, looking forward to those Kerala vibes. Catch you soon!

P1: Hey, bro, what’s up? Been ages since we chatted!
P2: Arre, yaar, I know! Just been swamped with work. How are you, man?
P1: All good here. Just chilling in Mumbai, missing our old hangouts. You free this weekend?
P2: Hmm, let me check. Diwali prep is on full swing at home, but I can squeeze in some time. What’s the plan?
P1: How about catching a movie at PVR? There’s this new Bollywood flick everyone’s talking about.
P2: Sounds good! Is it that one with Ranbir Kapoor? I’ve been wanting to watch it.
P1: Exactly that one! Let’s book tickets for Saturday evening. Cool with you?
P2: Perfect. And after, we can grab some vada pav near Marine Drive, na?
P1: Arre, now you’re talking! Nothing beats street food in Mumbai. I’m already drooling.
P2: Haha, same! Alright, I’ll confirm with the missus and text you by tomorrow.
P1: No worries, take your time. Just don’t back out last minute like last time!
P2: Haha, promise I won’t. Catch you soon, bro!

P1: Hey, Neha! How’ve you been?
P2: Hey, Riya! I’m good, just chilling. What about you?
P1: All good here. Listen, I was thinking we should plan a movie night soon. It’s been ages!
P2: Oh, totally! I’m so up for it. Maybe this weekend?
P1: Perfect. Let’s do Saturday evening. I’ll get some snacks—how about samosas and chai?
P2: Yum, that’s a must! I’ll bring some mithai too, since Diwali is around the corner.
P1: Nice! Let’s watch something light, maybe a Bollywood rom-com?
P2: Yes, I’m in the mood for something like that. How about we watch at my place?
P1: Sure, works for me. I’ll come by around 6. Should I call anyone else?
P2: Maybe just Aarti? Keep it small and cozy.
P1: Agreed. I’ll text her. Can’t wait for this!
P2: Same here! It’s gonna be so much fun. See you soon!

P1: Hey, dost, you free for a quick chat? Got some updates on the Bangalore project.
P2: Hey, yaar! Yeah, I’ve got a few minutes before my next call. What’s up?
P1: So, I just had a meeting with the team. We’re ahead of schedule by a week!
P2: Seriously? That’s amazing, bhai. How’d you manage that?
P1: Extra hours and a lot of coffee, man. The client is super happy, though.
P2: That’s what matters. You in Mumbai next week? We should celebrate.
P1: Yeah, I’ll be there on Tuesday. How about some vada pav at Gateway?
P2: Arre, you read my mind! Let’s do it. I’ll call up a few others too.
P1: Cool, make it a small gang. By the way, how’s your workload these days?
P2: Hectic, yaar. Got a deadline before Diwali, so no rest for me.
P1: Hang in there, bhai. After Diwali, we’ll plan a proper chill session.
P2: Can’t wait for that. Alright, I gotta jump on this call. Tuesday, Gateway, done!
P1: Done, man. Talk soon. Bye!
P2: Bye, take care!

P1: Hey, Di! How are you doing today?
P2: Hey, Sis! I’m good, just sipping some chai and relaxing. What about you?
P1: I’m alright, just finished some work. Are you coming to Mumbai for Diwali this year?
P2: Oh, I wish! But I’m stuck with office work. Maybe I’ll join for the last day of celebrations.
P1: Arre, that’s sad! We’re planning a big puja at home. Ma is already making lists for mithai.
P2: Haha, Ma and her lists! I’ll miss her special kaju katli. Send me some pics, na?
P1: Of course, I’ll spam you with all the rangoli and food pics. You better save some leave for next time!
P2: Deal! By the way, did you buy new clothes for the festival?
P1: Yes, got a pretty lehenga from Bandra. What about you?
P2: I’m thinking of ordering a saree online. Let’s video call and show each other, okay?
P1: Sure, let’s do it tomorrow evening. I can’t wait to see yours!
P2: Perfect! Alright, I’ve got to run now, some errands to do. Talk soon?
P1: Yeah, take care, Di. Love you!
P2: Love you too, Sis. Bye!

P1: Hey, man, you coming to the Diwali party at my place next week?
P2: Arre, wouldn’t miss it for the world! Your mom’s laddoos are legendary!
P1: Haha, true that! She’s already started prepping. Got any special requests?
P2: Just tell her to make extra kaju katli. I’m obsessed, yaar.
P1: Noted. By the way, we’re planning some card games too. You in for Teen Patti?
P2: Bro, I’m the Teen Patti king! Prepare to lose all your fake money!
P1: Haha, we’ll see about that. Also, thinking of setting up a small dance floor for Bollywood tracks.
P2: Awesome! I’m brushing up on my “Naatu Naatu” moves already. You better keep up!
P1: Challenge accepted! Oh, and bring your brother along if he’s free.
P2: Will do. He’s been asking about your epic parties. What time should we reach?
P1: Around 7 PM. We’ll start with some snacks and rangoli.
P2: Perfect. I’ll be there, man. Can’t wait for the festivities!
P1: Same here. See you soon, bro!

P1: Hi, Maa, how’s everything in Delhi?
P2: Beta, all good here! Just came back from the market, got some stuff for Diwali.
P1: Nice! Are you making your special kaju katli this time?
P2: Of course, how can Diwali happen without it? What about you, are you coming?
P1: I’m trying, Maa. Work is crazy, but I’ll book tickets soon.
P2: Don’t delay, beta. We all miss you here.
P1: I miss you too! How’s Papa’s health now?
P2: He’s better, but still grumbling about the cold weather.
P1: Haha, tell him to have more adrak wali chai!
P2: I’ll tell him that. When will you call next?
P1: Maybe tomorrow evening. I’ll update you on the tickets.
P2: Alright, take care, beta. Eat properly!
P1: Yes, Maa. Love you, bye!
P2: Love you too. Bye!

P1: Hello, Neha! Kya kar rahi ho?
P2: Hi, Vikram! Bas ghar pe hoon, thodi si cleaning kar rahi hoon. Aur tum?
P1: Arre, same yaar. Weekend pe thoda relax kar raha hoon. Mumbai ki traffic se thak gaya hoon.
P2: Haha, woh toh hai. Kabhi kabhi lagta hai chhoti jagah shift kar loon. Koi plan bana rahe ho?
P1: Haan, soch raha hoon next month ek short trip pe jaoon. Goa ya koi hill station. Chalegi?
P2: Goa sounds amazing! Kab tak plan kar rahe ho?
P1: November first week. Office se chhutti mil jayegi hopefully. Chal, saath mein chai peete hain aur plan banate hain.
P2: Theek hai, par chai tum banane wale ho, okay? Main bas enjoy karungi.
P1: Haha, no problem. Aur haan, koi acha restaurant pata hai wahan pe?
P2: Haan, ek-doh try kiye hain. Details bhej doongi. Aur tum koi seafood pasand karte ho na?
P1: Haan, bilkul! Chal, phir final karte hain jaldi. Baat karta hoon.
P2: Okay, cool. Bye Vikram!
P1: Bye Neha!

P1: Yo, dost, kya haal hai? Did you finish that report for the Delhi client?
P2: Hey, haan yaar, just mailed it to them this morning. Fingers crossed!
P1: Good job, man. Unka feedback mila kya abhi tak?
P2: Nahi, abhi toh bas ‘received’ ka reply aaya. Thoda nervous hoon.
P1: Arre, chill kar, tu ne solid kaam kiya hai. By the way, kal office aa raha hai kya?
P2: Haan, plan toh hai. Kyun, koi meeting hai?
P1: Nahi, bas socha lunch pe saath mein ek vada pav khaate hain, local style.
P2: Haha, yeh plan toh mast hai. Chal, done. Usual stall near the station?
P1: Haan, wahi. Aur ek cutting chai bhi order kar denge.
P2: Perfect, bhai. Chal, kal milte hain. Work pe koi update ho toh bata.
P1: Thik hai, will do. See you tomorrow!
P2: See you, yaar!

P1: Hi, I just got back from Mumbai yesterday. Missed you!
P2: Hey, missed you too! How was the trip? Tell me everything.
P1: It was crazy fun. Stayed near Marine Drive and had vada pav every day!
P2: Haha, living the Mumbai life! Did you catch the sea breeze there?
P1: Oh yes, evenings at Marine Drive were the best. So peaceful.
P2: Sounds amazing. Did you visit any other spots?
P1: Yeah, went to Gateway of India and took a ferry. Felt like a tourist!
P2: That’s classic Mumbai. How was the crowd? Always so hectic.
P1: Super crowded, but the energy is unreal. Also had cutting chai on the streets.
P2: Now I’m craving some. Did you shop at Colaba or something?
P1: Yes, got some funky earrings. You’d love the vibe there.
P2: I bet. Next time, I’m tagging along. Deal?
P1: Deal! We’ll explore Bandra together. I missed the cafes there.
P2: Can’t wait. Let’s plan soon over some chai at my place.
P1: Sounds good. I’ll bring some Mumbai stories with me!

P1: Hi, Arjun! What’s up?
P2: Hey, Meera! Just got back from work. What’s on your mind?
P1: I was thinking we should plan something for Diwali. Maybe a get-together?
P2: That’s a great idea! Where are you thinking of hosting it?
P1: Maybe at my place in Delhi. We can do rangoli and light diyas together.
P2: Sounds festive! Count me in. Should I bring some sweets?
P1: Oh, yes, please! Maybe some kaju katli? I’ll handle the snacks.
P2: Deal. Let’s also get some crackers, the eco-friendly ones.
P1: Good call! I’ll invite a few more friends too. You okay with that?
P2: Of course! The more, the merrier. When’s the plan?
P1: How about the evening before Diwali? I’ll confirm the date.
P2: Works for me. Can’t wait for the festivities!
P1: Same here! I’ll text you the details soon.
P2: Awesome. Talk later!

P1: Hey, Didi, how are you doing?
P2: I’m good, bhai! Just finished making some chai, want to come over?
P1: Haha, wish I could! I’m stuck in Mumbai traffic right now.
P2: Oh no, the usual mess near Bandra, huh?
P1: Exactly! Anyway, are you planning anything for Holi this year?
P2: Yeah, we’re hosting a small get-together. You should come with Bhabhi!
P1: Sounds fun! I’ll check with her and let you know.
P2: Cool, and bring some of those famous gujiyas from your side.
P1: Haha, deal! How’s everyone at home?
P2: All good. Ma is busy with her kitty party prep, as usual.
P1: Classic Ma! Alright, I’ll call later once I’m out of this jam.
P2: Okay, drive safe, bhai. Talk soon!
P1: Bye, Didi!

P1: Hello, Nani! How are you feeling today?
P2: Hello, beta! I’m doing okay, just a little tired. How are you, my dear?
P1: I’m good, Nani. I was thinking about you and your amazing halwa. Can I come over this weekend?
P2: Arre, of course, beta! I’ll make your favorite carrot halwa. Bring some elaichi from the market, okay?
P1: Sure, I’ll get it on the way. How’s the weather in Delhi? It’s so humid here in Chennai.
P2: It’s getting chilly here. I’ve started wearing my shawl in the evenings. You take care in that humidity, drink lots of water.
P1: I will, Nani. By the way, are you planning anything for Navratri?
P2: Yes, we’re having a small dandiya night in the colony. You should come if you’re free!
P1: Oh, that sounds fun! I’ll try to make it. Let me check my schedule and confirm.
P2: No hurry, beta. Just let me know. I’ll keep some garba steps ready to teach you!
P1: Haha, I can’t wait! Alright, Nani, I’ll call you tomorrow with the plan.
P2: Okay, take care, beta. Talk soon.
P1: Bye, Nani. Love you!
P2: Love you too, my child. Bye!

P1: Hey, Priya! Just got a message that my Amazon order is out for delivery today!
P2: Oh, nice, Anjali! What did you order? Something special?
P1: Yeah, I got a saree for Diwali. Been eyeing it for weeks.
P2: Wow, that’s exciting! What color is it? I’m looking for one too.
P1: It’s a deep maroon with gold zari work. Perfect for the puja.
P2: Sounds stunning! Can’t wait to see you in it. Got matching jewelry?
P1: Not yet, yaar. Planning to borrow some from my mom. What about you?
P2: Haha, same! Moms always have the best stuff. Where’s the delivery guy now?
P1: Tracker says he’s in Andheri. Should be here in an hour, I think.
P2: Great! Let me know when it arrives. We can chat over chai later.
P1: Definitely! I’ll send you pics once I try it on. Deal?
P2: Deal! I’m so excited for Diwali this year. Let’s plan something fun!
P1: Absolutely. Maybe a small get-together at my place? We’ll see.
P2: Sounds perfect. Okay, I’ll let you go. Update me soon!
P1: Will do, Priya. Talk to you in a bit. Bye!
P2: Bye, Anjali!

P1: Hey, bro, how’s it going? Just wanted to give you a quick update on the project.
P2: Hey, man! All good here. What’s the news? You sound excited.
P1: Yeah, yaar, I finally finished the presentation for the Delhi client. Sent it over this morning.
P2: Nice! How did they take it? Any feedback yet?
P1: Not yet, but I’m hoping for the best. Fingers crossed, bhai. Spent all night on it.
P2: Arre, I’m sure they’ll love it. You always kill it with those slides. Want to grab some chai later to de-stress?
P1: Sounds good, man. How about that stall near Andheri station around 5?
P2: Perfect. I’ll be there. By the way, did you start prepping for the Diwali bonus meeting?
P1: Not yet, yaar. I’ll get to it tomorrow. Too much on my plate right now.
P2: Haha, same here. Let’s brainstorm over chai. I’ve got some ideas.
P1: Deal. See you at 5 then. Don’t be late like last time!
P2: Arre, that was just once! I’ll be there, don’t worry. Catch you later, bhai.
P1: Haha, cool. Bye!

P1: Hey, Sneha! Just got a call from the Swiggy Instamart guy. My groceries are on the way.
P2: Hey, Riya! That’s quick. Ordering for something special today?
P1: Yeah, making some sweets for Diwali. Thought I’d get started early.
P2: Nice! Are you making laddoos or something else?
P1: Laddoos and some kaju katli. Gotta impress the family, na?
P2: Haha, totally! Your kaju katli is the best. Save some for me, okay?
P1: Of course, yaar. I’ll pack a box for you. When can you drop by?
P2: Maybe tomorrow? I’m in Mumbai till then. Got some shopping to do.
P1: Perfect. Come over in the evening. We’ll have chai and gossip.
P2: Love that plan! By the way, did the delivery guy say how long he’ll take?
P1: He said 20 minutes max. Hope he doesn’t get stuck in traffic near Bandra.
P2: Fingers crossed! Let me know if you need help with anything.
P1: Thanks, Sneha. I’ll manage, but I’ll call if I need a hand.
P2: Cool. Talk later then. Can’t wait for those sweets!
P1: Haha, you’ll get them. Bye for now!
P2: Bye, Riya!

P1: Hey, Anjali! Long time, how are you doing?
P2: Hey, Kavita! I’m good, just busy with work. What about you?
P1: Same here, but I need a break. How about a picnic at Sanjay Gandhi National Park this weekend?
P2: That sounds so relaxing! I’m totally in. When are we going?
P1: Let’s do Sunday morning. We can catch the early breeze.
P2: Perfect. I’ll make some poha for breakfast. What are you bringing?
P1: I’ll get some chai in a flask and a few biscuits. Old-school vibes!
P2: Haha, love that! Should we invite anyone else?
P1: Maybe just one or two friends. I’ll ask Shreya if she’s free.
P2: Okay, cool. I’ll pack a mat and some games like cards.
P1: Nice idea! It’s gonna be a chill day, just what I need.
P2: Same here. Can’t wait to catch up properly.
P1: Me too. I’ll confirm the plan tomorrow.
P2: Sounds good. Talk later!

P1: Hey, Karan, how are you, bro?
P2: Hey, Nikhil, I’m good, yaar! Just got back from a boring lecture at uni. What about you?
P1: Haha, same old. Preparing for my final year exams. Total stress mode!
P2: I know that feeling, man. Which subject’s giving you a hard time?
P1: Accounts, bhai. Numbers just don’t make sense sometimes.
P2: Haha, I’m no expert, but I can help if you want. Let’s sit with some chai and figure it out.
P1: That’d be awesome. There’s this nice tapri near my place in Thane. Wanna come over this weekend?
P2: Sounds good. I’m free on Saturday. Let’s do it.
P1: Perfect. Also, did you hear about the college fest next month? They’re planning a big Holi bash.
P2: Yeah, man! I’m totally in for that. Gotta stock up on colors and pichkaris already.
P1: Haha, same! Let’s make a plan with the group. I’ll start a WhatsApp thread.
P2: Cool, do that. Anyway, I’ll see you Saturday. Study hard till then!
P1: Will try, bhai. Catch you soon!
P2: Take care, man!

P1: Hey, Riya! Long time, yaar. How’s everything with you?
P2: Hey, Anu! I know, right? I’m good, just super busy with wedding preps for my cousin. What’s up with you?
P1: Oh, wedding season already! I’m just chilling, planning a small trip to Goa with friends. You in?
P2: Arre, I wish! But I’m stuck with all the shaadi shopping in Ahmedabad. Maybe after the wedding?
P1: No worries, we’ll plan something later. So, what’s the menu looking like for the wedding?
P2: Oh, it’s going to be epic! Gujarati thali, chaat counters, and even a live jalebi stall. You have to come!
P1: Wow, that sounds amazing! I’ll definitely try to make it. Send me the dates, na?
P2: Sure, I’ll WhatsApp you the invite tonight. By the way, are you making anything special for Dussehra?
P1: Yeah, Ma and I are planning to make fafda and jalebi at home. Old-school vibes!
P2: That’s so sweet! Save some for me if I visit, okay?
P1: Of course, yaar! Alright, I’ve got to go now, let’s catch up again soon.
P2: Yes, let’s! Take care, Anu. Talk later!
P1: Bye, Riya. Take care!
P2: Bye!

P1: Hey, bhai, how’s it going? Just wanted to check on that project update.
P2: Arre, good to hear from you, man! Yeah, project is almost done, just final touches left.
P1: That’s great news! Client ke saath meeting hui kya?
P2: Haan, yesterday. They’re happy with the progress, bas thoda deadline pe pressure hai.
P1: Arre, tension mat le, you’ll manage. By the way, Diwali ke liye koi plan?
P2: Not yet, yaar. Maybe family ke saath ghar pe hi celebrate karunga. Tu bata?
P1: Same here, bhai. Mumbai mein traffic se bachne ke liye ghar pe hi mithai and lights.
P2: Haha, sahi hai. Chal, let’s catch up after Diwali, maybe ek chai pe?
P1: Pakka, bhai. I’ll call you. Work pe update dena if anything urgent.
P2: Done, don’t worry. Take care, yaar!
P1: Tu bhi, bhai. Bye!

P1: Yo, dude, did you catch the latest episode of that web series on Netflix?
P2: Which one, yaar? I’m hooked on like three right now!
P1: The one based in Delhi, with all the political drama. Totally gripping, man!
P2: Oh, yeah, “Capital Chaos”! Bro, that last episode was insane. What a twist!
P1: Right? I didn’t see that coming at all. Wanna chat about it over some chai tomorrow?
P2: Sure, let’s meet at that tapri near my place. Their cutting chai is legit.
P1: Done. What time works for you? I’m free post-lunch.
P2: Let’s say 4 PM? I’ve got a quick meeting before that.
P1: Cool, 4 it is. By the way, you got any Diwali plans yet?
P2: Not really, just family stuff. Might go to my cousin’s place in Pune. You?
P1: Probably sticking around in the city. Let’s plan something if you’re here!
P2: Definitely, yaar. We’ll figure it out. See you tomorrow for chai!
P1: Can’t wait. Take care, bro!

P1: Hey, Riya, kya haal hai? Long time no talk!
P2: Arre, Rohan! Sab theek hai. Bas, thoda busy thi. How are you?
P1: I’m good, yaar. Just thought of catching up. Diwali ke plans kya hain?
P2: Abhi toh decide nahi kiya. Maybe ghar pe hi family ke saath. Aur tu?
P1: Same here. But soch raha hoon friends ke saath bhi ek chhota get-together kar loon. Join karegi?
P2: Sounds fun! Kab aur kahan?
P1: Shayad Diwali ke next day. Mere ghar pe. Chai aur mithai on me!
P2: Haha, deal! Main kuch snacks le aati hoon. Diwali ke laddoo bhi banaye hain ghar pe.
P1: Wah, perfect! Aur haan, koi naya Bollywood movie dekha recently?
P2: Haan, last week ek dekhi thi, par yaad nahi naam. Tu recommend kar na kuch!
P1: Chalo, phir hum saath mein dekh lenge after Diwali. Cool?
P2: Bilkul! Bas mujhe popcorn chahiye, okay?
P1: Haha, done. Chal, phir baad mein baat karte hain. Take care!
P2: Okay, bye Rohan!

P1: Hey Neha, just got an update from Zomato. My food order is almost here!
P2: Hey Arjun, that’s great! What did you order? I’m hungry just thinking about it.
P1: Haha, got some biryani from that famous place in Hyderabad. Craving it!
P2: Wow, Hyderabad biryani? You’re making me jealous! Is it for lunch?
P1: Yeah, thought I’d treat myself today. Long week at work, you know.
P2: Totally get it. Did the app say how long till it reaches?
P1: Should be here in 10 minutes. Hope the delivery guy doesn’t get stuck in traffic.
P2: Fingers crossed! Pune traffic can be a mess sometimes.
P1: Tell me about it! Hey, if you’re free, want to join me for some?
P2: I wish! But I’m out with family. Maybe we can order again for Diwali?
P1: That’s a plan! We’ll get extra and have a proper feast.
P2: Absolutely! Let me know how the biryani tastes.
P1: Will do. I’ll call you later with the review.
P2: Haha, looking forward to it. Talk soon, Arjun.
P1: Bye, Neha!
P2: Bye!

P1: Hey, Neha, kaisi ho? It’s been ages since we talked!
P2: Arre, Priya! I’m good, yaar, just busy with work. How are you?
P1: All good here. Just planning some Diwali shopping. You got any plans for the festival?
